page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-32-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the c4d server family. It features 32 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs) and 124.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 3.88 GB of RAM per core. The instance configuration includes two local solid-state drives (SSDs) for local storage requirements, while providing zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Operating with dedicated CPU allocation, this server type delivers consistent processing capabilities without resource sharing. The ratio of approximately 4 GB of memory per vCPU positions the hardware profile as a balanced resource option. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the combination of dedicated compute cores and integrated local SSD storage provides a consolidated resource density suitable for workloads requiring direct, low-latency storage access without the overhead of external network-attached storage.

Given the 32 vCPUs and 124.0 GB RAM configuration, this server is designed for general-purpose applications, multi-threaded processing tasks, and database workloads that benefit from local SSD performance. It is well-suited for web serving, containerized microservices, and medium-scale enterprise applications requiring a stable balance of compute and memory resources.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-32-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the c4d family, configured with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 124.0 GB of RAM, providing 3.88 GB of memory per core. The instance features two local SSDs to support high-speed local storage needs, though it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. With its dedicated CPU allocation, the server delivers stable, non-shared processing performance. This balanced resource profile makes it qualitatively cost-effective for workloads requiring a steady ratio of compute to memory alongside direct-attached storage. It is highly suitable for general-purpose enterprise applications, database hosting, and web serving. The dedicated core design ensures predictable execution times, making it a reliable choice for production environments that demand consistent throughput without resource contention. Additionally, the absence of bundled public IPv4 addresses highlights a design optimized for internal VPC deployments.
